data.scene: The user enables BLE beacon monitoring in sensor settings; the app passively scans for nearby BLE iBeacons using the AltBeacon library.
data.purpose: Enable Bluetooth-based presence detection and beacon-triggered automations in Home Assistant.
data.source: AltBeacon library BLE scan (Android BluetoothAdapter / BluetoothLeScanner)
data.item: Detected iBeacon UUID, major/minor values, estimated distance in meters, RSSI signal strength, beacon MAC addresses
data.frequency: Continuous background BLE scan (update on each beacon detection cycle)
data.thirdparty: AltBeacon library used for BLE scanning; detected beacon data is sent only to the user's self-hosted Home Assistant server
data.switchpath: Settings -> Sensor Management -> Bluetooth Sensors -> Beacon Monitoring -> Enable/Disable

permissions.name: BLUETOOTH_SCAN
permissions.scene: When enabling BLE beacon monitoring on Android 12+
permissions.purpose: Scan for nearby BLE iBeacons for presence detection and beacon-triggered automations
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE beacon monitoring will not detect nearby beacons

permissions.name: BLUETOOTH_CONNECT
permissions.scene: When enabling Bluetooth connection sensor on Android 12+
permissions.purpose: Read paired/connected Bluetooth device names and MAC addresses for presence detection
permissions.impact: If denied, Bluetooth connection sensor will not report paired/connected device data

permissions.name: BLUETOOTH_ADVERTISE
permissions.scene: When enabling BLE transmitter on Android 12+
permissions.purpose: Broadcast the device as a BLE iBeacon so other devices can detect it for presence detection
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE transmitter will not be able to advertise the device as a beacon

permissions.name: ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
permissions.scene: When enabling BLE beacon monitoring on Android 9-11 (required by Android for BLE scans)
permissions.purpose: Enable BLE beacon scanning (required by Android platform for Bluetooth LE scans prior to Android 12)
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE beacon scanning will not be available on Android 9-11 devices

data.scene: The user enables the BLE transmitter in Bluetooth sensor settings; the app broadcasts a user-configured iBeacon identifier via BLE advertising.
data.purpose: Allow other Home Assistant companion devices or third-party BLE scanners to detect this device as a known iBeacon for presence detection.
data.item: User-configured iBeacon UUID, major/minor values (broadcast outward; no data read from device)
data.frequency: Continuous BLE advertising while transmitter is enabled
data.processing: Broadcast only (no data uploaded to server)
data.recipient: Any nearby BLE scanner scanning for the configured iBeacon pattern
data.thirdparty: No third-party involvement; the user configures the beacon identity and broadcasts it locally
data.switchpath: Settings -> Sensor Management -> Bluetooth Sensors -> BLE Transmitter -> Enable/Disable

permissions.name: BLUETOOTH_SCAN
permissions.scene: When enabling BLE beacon monitoring on Android 12+
permissions.purpose: Scan for nearby BLE iBeacons for presence detection and beacon-triggered automations
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE beacon monitoring will not detect nearby beacons

permissions.purpose: Read paired/connected Bluetooth device names and MAC addresses for presence detection
permissions.impact: If denied, Bluetooth connection sensor will not report paired/connected device data

permissions.purpose: Broadcast the device as a BLE iBeacon so other devices can detect it for presence detection
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE transmitter will not be able to advertise the device as a beacon

permissions.name: ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
permissions.scene: When enabling BLE beacon monitoring on Android 9-11 (required by Android for BLE scans)
permissions.purpose: Enable BLE beacon scanning (required by Android platform for Bluetooth LE scans prior to Android 12)
permissions.impact: If denied, BLE beacon scanning will not be available on Android 9-11 devices

data.scene: The app periodically reads paired and connected Bluetooth devices via Android BluetoothAdapter.
data.purpose: Provide a list of paired/connected Bluetooth devices for presence detection and automation rules based on device connections.
data.item: Paired Bluetooth device names and MAC addresses, currently connected GATT device names and MAC addresses
data.frequency: Periodic and on Bluetooth state change
data.thirdparty: No third-party data sharing; data sent to the user's self-hosted Home Assistant server. BLUETOOTH_CONNECT permission required on Android 12+.
data.switchpath: Settings -> Sensor Management -> Bluetooth Sensors -> Bluetooth Connection Sensor -> Enable/Disable
